The Big Picture

System Map

Click any node to expand it. Arrows show how each component relates to the next.

  • Entry Point sends requests to the Core System.
  • The Core System persists data via the Data Subsystem.
  • The Data Subsystem contains a Data Access layer that reads and writes the Data Model.

Component

Entry Point

Describe what this component does, the boundary it owns, and how it forwards work to the core system. Reference the general-purpose name only.

Component

Core System

Explain the core responsibility, the sub-systems it owns, and the relationships that make the pattern work.

System

Data Subsystem

This system was collapsed in the main map to keep the diagram readable. Inside, a data-access layer reads and writes a plain data model.

Component

Data Access

Explain the persistence boundary: the interface it exposes and how callers stay ignorant of the backing store.

Component

Data Model

Explain the shape of the data, how it is created and persisted, and which components depend on it.
